title: Fixing Inconsistent CSS Grid Row Height Calculation

statement.inputFormat: CSS grid layout definition as a string
statement.outputFormat: CSS grid layout definition as a string
statement.notes: Ensure that the grid rows have equal heights.

examples.explanation: Grid rows have equal heights, but with auto height

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The grid rows have inconsistent heights because the grid-template-rows property is set to repeat(2, 1fr). To fix this, set the grid-template-rows property to repeat(2, auto).
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution involves manually calculating the height of each grid row.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution involves using the repeat() function to set the grid-template-rows property.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution involves setting the grid-template-rows property to repeat(2, auto).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution ensures that the grid rows have equal heights.
editorial.dryRun:
Step-by-step example run: 

1. Set the grid-template-rows property to repeat(2, auto).
2. Ensure that the grid rows have equal heights.
commonMistakes: Not setting the grid-template-rows property to repeat(2, auto).
